titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Kelly Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Laura Tatum, Melissa Doerken, Gene Cartwright, and Amina Blacksher

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday October 6, 2018 at 08:48
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.abstract: The papers include documentation for many of Richard Kelly's projects, including layouts and detailed working drawings, details of lighting fixtures, correspondence and architectural design drawings, and financial records. They also include correspondence with various institutions including universities and technical societies.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Scott Kelly, 2005; Alex von Bidder, 2012; Addison and Scott Kelly, 2014.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in three series: I. Project files, 1939-1977. II. Professional papers, 1909-1976. III. Personal papers, 1925-1981.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by Scott Kelly and Addison Kelly for materials authored or otherwise produced by their father Richard Kelly. After their lifetimes or May 1, 2054, their copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard Kelly Papers (MS 1838). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Richard Kelly, architectural lighting designer, was born in Zanesville, Ohio, on September 22, 1910. After graduating from Columbia University in 1932, he opened an office in New York to design and sell architectural lighting. He enrolled in the architecture program at Yale University, where he also studied with theatrical lighting designer, Stanley McCandless, graduating with a B.F.A. in 1942. After working for several architectural offices, he opened his own lighting design firm in New York in or around 1947. Kelly developed a new vocabulary for architectural lighting, exploring the ways in which light affects mood, and identifying the concepts of focal glow, ambient luminescence, and the play of brilliants. During his career, Kelly worked with many noted architects, including Louis Kahn, Ludwig Mies van der Rohe, Harrison and Abramovitz, Eero Saarinen, I. M. Pei, and Philip Johnson. Among his notable projects are: the Seagram Building and Lincoln Center in New York, the Kimbell Art Museum in Fort Worth, Texas, and the Yale Center for British Art in New Haven, Connecticut. In addition to his own practice, Kelly lectured on lighting design at Yale University, Harvard University, Cooper Union, Columbia, and other architectural schools and organizations. He died in 1977.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers include documentation for many of Richard Kelly's projects, including layouts and detailed working drawings, details of lighting fixtures, correspondence and architectural design drawings, and financial records. They also include correspondence with various institutions, including universities and technical societies, as well as some personal papers and student work.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: For a list of projects and the boxes in which project materials are located, please consult theProject Index.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Richard Kelly recorded daily appointments, activities, and events in both day books and appointment books.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes information about lamp designs by Richard Kelly.

did.unittitle: Middletown Manufacturing, orders and inquiries regarding lights designed by Kelly
Client list card file, with billing information

odd.head: General
odd.p: Slides are unlabeled and undated. Many depict architectural lighting projects on which Richard Kelly worked, as well as miscellaneous images. There were project and subject label markers which had been dislodged from their positions before the materials were received by Manuscripts and Archives; the markers were retained in box 410, folder 47.

did.unittitle: Building Research Institute (BRI) Building Lighting Committee
Flowering Plant Societies of New York
Indoor Light Gardening Society of America (ILGSA)
"The Better to See," House and Garden
"Choosing the Right Light for Your Plants," The Designer
"Design Journal: Private Lighting," Interior Design and Decoration
"A Home of Your Own for Perfect Living"
"How to Light Your Paintings," House and Garden
"Making the Most of Lighting," New York Times
"The Meaning of Light in Architectural Design"
"Sales Control Through Engineered Lighting Effects," Women's Wear Daily
"Subliminal Store Lighting Can Create Positive Reaction Among Customers"

odd.head: General
odd.p: Some of the items in these folders have not been definitively identified as student work.
